title: Add durable Facial command degraded and error receipt envelopes
operator_request: MT-029 exposed success command receipts and HTTP error paths. Durable degraded/error command envelopes remain needed so models can recover from command failures without scraping generic HTTP errors.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Do not reintroduce parser-only degraded/error states. Backend must produce the envelope shape before frontend accepts it.

scope.summary: Make Facial command failures and degraded outcomes produce durable, model-usable command envelopes and receipt artifacts instead of only generic HTTP error bodies.
base_scope: Define a Facial command failure/degraded envelope that carries command, actor when available, status, error code, recovery hint, result schema if relevant, and source artifact refs.
Persist failure/degraded command receipt artifacts through ArtifactStore when the request has enough context to do so safely.
Keep missing or malformed actor/header failures as normal HTTP validation errors unless a safe actor placeholder and audit policy are explicitly implemented.
Update backend Facial command routes so semantic blocked/degraded states return the command envelope shape instead of parser-invisible generic errors.
Update frontend parser/tests to accept only backend-produced statuses and require correct artifacts when a receipt is promised.
acceptance_criteria: At least one semantic blocked path, such as export without allow_partial while undecided items remain, returns a structured Facial command envelope and receipt artifact.
Frontend parser accepts degraded/blocked/error statuses only after backend tests prove those statuses are produced.
Failure receipts include actor attribution when an actor header is present, command name, status, error code, recovery hint, and source/session refs where available.
HTTP transport status and command-envelope status are documented in tests so models know which failures are parser-visible and which remain transport validation errors.
No generic non-Facial ArtifactStore JSON read surface is introduced.
risks_and_controls: Risk: models confuse malformed transport errors with command-level failures. Control: keep extractor/header failures as HTTP errors unless explicitly envelope-wrapped.
Risk: failure envelopes write misleading receipts without enough context. Control: only persist receipts after command, actor, and session/artifact context are known.
Risk: frontend accepts statuses the backend still cannot produce. Control: parser tests must pair every accepted status with a backend route proof.
verification: Backend route test for a produced blocked/degraded/error envelope and persisted receipt artifact.
Frontend parser test that accepts only statuses proven by backend route tests.
Artifact read negative test remains in place for non-Facial JSON payloads.

review_lenses: transport error versus command error separation

manual_requirement: Required if this MT exposes or changes operator/model-visible Facial failure behavior.

handoff.note: Created during MT-029 closeout after post-review found parser-only degraded/blocked/error states. MT-029 was narrowed to live success envelopes plus HTTP error paths; this MT owns durable failure envelope parity.
